summary: Round 10 reopens HNSW from the round-9 authority improvement rather than from a hard stop. Round 9 proved that production search-path cleanup can materially raise Rust HNSW to 1845.608 qps, but native still leads by about 5.61x on the same-schema lane. The next explicit hypothesis is layer0_slab_locality: co-locate layer-0 neighbors and vectors for the production fast path and test whether memory locality closes more of the remaining gap.
action: Keep the historical HNSW family verdict unchanged, freeze the round-9 authority improvement as the round-10 baseline, and require a fresh slab-layout audit plus another same-schema authority rerun before making any broader HNSW performance claim.
